text:
Incredible flying taxis could ferry people to the Olympics for £250 each.

German company Volocopter manufactures the "VoloCity", an electric aircraft aiming to be used at the Paris Olympics this summer.

The VoloCity is a two-seater aircraft (including the pilot's seat) equipped with batteries powering 18 rotors arranged in a ring above the cockpit.

To carry paying passengers, the VoloCity will have to obtain certification from the European Aviation Safety Agency (EASA).

Promotors are hoping to try them out during the Olympic and Paralympic Games, but aiming for certification by the European authorities in the autumn.

READ MORE:Eurostar tipped for huge new multi-million plan to add more routes



The VoloCity has been described by Volocopter as a “game changer” that will transport people between transport hubs “whisper-quiet and emission-free” for “seamless, faster, and more convenient options”.

The Paris airports operator, ADP Group, has joined forces with the Île-de-France region and Volocopter to demonstrate the feasibility of this new mode of transport, which is designed to link different take-off and landing sites known as "vertiports".

There are four vertiports in operation - Paris-Charles-de-Gaulle and Le Bourget airports, the Issy-les-Moulineaux heliport to the south-west of Paris and the Saint-Cyr-l'Ecole airfield near Versailles.

A fifth site, a barge on the Seine at Austerlitz, has yet to receive the go-ahead from the Ministry of Transport, but "it's ready, moored, positioned".

Don't miss:The £68m bridge that stretches 666 metres and breathes fire

"It's an aeronautical programme, so we know it's complex," explained Jean-Christophe Drai, Volocopter's manager for France.

"Sometimes you discover things as you go along. So yes, there has been a bit of a delay. We're still aiming to obtain this certification in the autumn."

Edward Arkwright, executive director of ADP said: "We won't have any paying passengers during the Olympic and Paralympic Games. But our objective remains to have a fare-paying passenger before the end of the year.”

Volocopter began selling 1,000 tickets for VoloCity air taxi rides in Paris in 2020 for £255 each with the flight time to be a 15-minute spin.

title: The brand new incredible flying taxis which cost £250 a ride | World | News | Express.co.uk
author: Grace Piercy, Maria Ortega
description: Incredible flying taxis could ferry people to the Paris Olympics for £250 each.
